headline: Oil Prices Fall, Despite Iran's 14-Point 'Fabrication'; Airline Stocks Fly
summary: Oil prices retreated close to $90 a barrel as markets bet that a U.S.-Iran deal is likely happen soon.

content.title: Oil Prices Fall, Despite Iran's 14-Point 'Fabrication'; Airline Stocks Fly
content.summary: Oil prices retreated close to $90 a barrel as markets bet that a U.S.-Iran deal is likely happen soon.

headline: Airline stocks rally as oil retreat eases fuel cost pressure
summary: Oil is down 8% over the last week as reports circulated that Washington and Tehran were edging closer to an agreement that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz. That drop helped push the S&P 500 and Nasdaq to record closing highs on Tuesday, underscoring the inverse relationship between crude prices and risk assets that has defined markets since the conflict began.

content.title: Airline stocks rally as oil retreat eases fuel cost pressure
content.summary: Oil is down 8% over the last week as reports circulated that Washington and Tehran were edging closer to an agreement that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz. headline: Stock Index Futures Climb on AI Optimism and U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Hopes
summary: June S&P 500 E-Mini futures (ESM26) are up +0.36%, and June Nasdaq 100 E-Mini futures (NQM26) are up +0.56% this morning as optimism surrounding artificial intelligence and hopes for a U.S.-Iran peace deal boosted sentiment.

content.title: Stock Index Futures Climb on AI Optimism and U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Hopes
content.summary: June S&P 500 E-Mini futures (ESM26) are up +0.36%, and June Nasdaq 100 E-Mini futures (NQM26) are up +0.56% this morning as optimism surrounding artificial intelligence and hopes for a U.S.-Iran peace deal boosted sentiment.

headline: Wall Street hangs near its records as oil prices ease
summary: U.S. stocks are hanging near their records Wednesday as oil prices fall and ease the pressure on households and businesses worldwide.  Stocks of companies with big fuel bills helped lead the way on hopes that lower oil prices will remove a big drag on their profits.  Delta Air Lines rose 4.1% and is on track to set an all-time high.

content.title: Wall Street hangs near its records as oil prices ease
content.summary: U.S. stocks are hanging near their records Wednesday as oil prices fall and ease the pressure on households and businesses worldwide.  Stocks of companies with big fuel bills helped lead the way on hopes that lower oil prices will remove a big drag on their profits.  Delta Air Lines rose 4.1% and is on track to set an all-time high.

thumbnail.caption: Trader Edward Curran, left, and specialist Meric Greenbaum, center, work on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange, Friday, May 22, 2026. (AP Photo/Richard Drew)

headline: Marvell, United, Zscaler, and These Stocks Explain the Market Today
summary: Oil prices, meanwhile, continued to decline after Iran’s Revolutionary Guard Corps said the possibility of a return to war with the U.S. was “low.”  Optimism around the U.S. and Iran reaching a peace agreement, despite ongoing hostilities, appeared to be growing.  The chip and tech stocks that have powered the market to record highs in recent weeks were mixed Wednesday.

content.title: Marvell, United, Zscaler, and These Stocks Explain the Market Today
content.summary: Oil prices, meanwhile, continued to decline after Iran’s Revolutionary Guard Corps said the possibility of a return to war with the U.S. was “low.”  Optimism around the U.S. and Iran reaching a peace agreement, despite ongoing hostilities, appeared to be growing.  The chip and tech stocks that have powered the market to record highs in recent weeks were mixed Wednesday.

headline: UAL Stock Gains Over 6% — And There Is A Starlink Connection
summary: United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby said the airline is accelerating cabin upgrades and expects Starlink internet across its ‘whole fleet’ by next year.

content.title: UAL Stock Gains Over 6% — And There Is A Starlink Connection

headline: US Airlines See Robust Demand Even as Consumer Confidence Falls
summary: (Bloomberg) -- American Airlines Group Inc. said it continues to see strong demand for travel, even as surging jet fuel drives up ticket prices and consumer confidence in the US edges down. Rival United Airlines Holdings Inc. also said demand continued to be robust. headline: US stocks inch to more records after oil prices drop
summary: U.S. stocks inched to more records Wednesday after oil prices fell and eased the pressure on households and businesses worldwide.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 182 points, or 0.4%, and the Nasdaq composite gained 0.1% as both indexes also set records.  Stocks of companies with big fuel bills helped lead the way on hopes that lower oil prices will remove a big drag on their profits.
